description: Cross-domain bibliography and source index for the psychology v2 bundle. Includes textbooks, handbook-style references, library-oriented resources, official guidelines, and evidence infrastructure.

referencePolicy.note: Use bibliographic metadata and source hierarchy; do not include copyrighted chapters or proprietary assessment items.

openAndOfficialSources.title: APA Guidelines for the Undergraduate Psychology Major 3.0
openAndOfficialSources.notes: Useful for curriculum goals and learning outcomes.

openAndOfficialSources.title: APA Competency Benchmarks / Benchmarks Evaluation System
APA Standards of Accreditation for Health Service Psychology
openAndOfficialSources.notes: Useful for training structure and competence expectations.

openAndOfficialSources.title: NIH OBSSR mixed methods and behavioral/social sciences research resources
openAndOfficialSources.notes: Useful for research design and mixed-methods rigor.

openAndOfficialSources.title: OpenStax Psychology 2e
openAndOfficialSources.notes: Useful for baseline concepts and plain-language refreshers.

openAndOfficialSources.title: NCBI Bookshelf behavioral and social sciences volumes

texts: APA Handbook of Research Methods in Psychology
Siegler et al. — How Children Develop
Shadish, Cook, & Campbell — Experimental and Quasi-Experimental Designs
Kazdin — Research Design in Clinical Psychology
Stanovich — Rationality and the Reflective Mind
Hayes, Strosahl, & Wilson — ACT basics/clinical frame

assessmentFamilies.restriction: task paradigms describable; proprietary implementation details may vary

examples: wellbeing, stress, affect, personality, social attitudes
assessmentFamilies.restriction: do not embed proprietary items or keys unless openly licensed

examples: executive-function tasks, attachment interviews, observational coding
assessmentFamilies.restriction: use descriptive metadata, not restricted administration kits
assistant may discuss purpose and limits but must not diagnose or self-administer restricted tools as a substitute for care

evidenceRules: Prefer systematic reviews/meta-analyses for intervention efficacy claims.
Separate construct validity, predictive validity, and clinical utility.
Mark classic studies as historically important even when modern replication debates exist.
For assistant output, keep mechanism claims proportional to evidence strength.

entryConditions: route through clinical safety first for crisis, diagnosis, self-harm, mania/psychosis, eating-disorder, or overdose language
notes: Prefer bounded explanation over diagnostic certainty.
Escalate to safety-first handling when risk signals are present.
Use source index for trust weighting and bibliographic routing.
